lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Good day to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What brings you here?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: The eternal quest for knowledge, of course! The name's Elias White. I'm something of an explorer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pleased to meet you. I'm [player name]. So you're searching for knowledge in an inn?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Not exactly. In fact, I was previously hunting down information on an ancient race deep within the desert.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: However, I received an interesting yet concerning tip- off from a contact of mine regarding some of my research. I came here to investigate for myself.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Oh, hello there. You look familiar. Have we met before?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so. The name's [player name]. You're Elias, right? You had a camp out near Uzer.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Ah, of course. You offered to help with my work, didn't you? I'm still very keen to take you up on that offer at some point, but a more pressing matter has delayed that somewhat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that what's brought you here?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Indeed. I recived an interesting yet concerning tip-off from a contact of mine regarding some of my research. I came here to investigate for myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything I can help with?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: How kind of you to offer. I'm not in need of any extra help right now though.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: How kind of you to offer. I definitely wouldn't complain about some extra support, if you're sure you're interested?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Absolutely! So what was this tip-off you received?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: It's nothing much, but apparently one of the patrons in here was ranting and raving about zombies. Most just dismissed him as having had one too many, but I'm not so sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Varrock has an interesting history when it comes to zombies. Tell me, what do you know of Arrav?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrav? As in the Shield of Arrav? I was the one who helped return it to the museum.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Ah, I heard about that. Excellent work! You may well know then that the former owner of that shield, Arrav himself, once defended Varrock against a zombie invasion led by a Mahjarrat named Zemouregal.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Arrav sacrificed himself to save his city, but Zemouregal survived.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you think these zombie sightings mean he's returned?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Apparently the zombies were seen forming ranks. Zombies don't naturally organise themselves, so someone must be organising them. I can think of worse candidates than Zemouregal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can see why you think this is worth looking into. Where do we start?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Zombies are not particularly light on their feet, so my hope is we can pick up their trail. The sightings were apparently right outside the inn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Follow the tracks and see where they came from. Good plan.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Then shall we get started?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ready when you are.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Excellent! I'm right behind you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's get going.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Lead the way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about Arrav and Zemouregal?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: It's quite the tale and I doubt I'd be able to do it justice, but I can try and give you an overview.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Back in the Fourth Age, a group of travellers came upon an abandoned child. It is said the child was unusual for one so young. Dark eyes and a fierce countenance, yet fair hair and skin, with a kind smile.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: The travellers saw this as an omen and set up camp where they found him. The camp soon grew into the town of Avarrocka, or Varrock as we now call it.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: As for the child, he grew to become a mighty hero. After defending the town from a goblin attack, he took on the name Arrav, a curse word in goblin tongue, for he had become the Curse of Goblins.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Arrav's heroic deeds continued, until he was granted a vision of the future. He saw the Mahjarrat Zemouregal claiming Avarrocka as his own.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Determined to stop Zemouregal in any way he could, Arrav set out to find a powerful weapon with which to best him. His journey took him to the Imcando Dwarves, who granted him a powerful shield.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Shield of Arrav?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Exactly. With the shield in hand, Arrav returned home and faced off against Zemouregal and his zombie horde. Thanks to him, the invasion was repelled, but at the cost of Arrav's life.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Zemouregal has not been seen since, but it is said that his last act before retreating was to claim Arrav's body, promising to turn him into an undead slave to one day return with.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Hundreds of years have now passed, and while there are few who haven't heard of Arrav, many have forgotten Zemouregal and his promise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what exactly is it you're researching?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: The Mahjarrat. It's fair to say they are quite the subject of fascination for me. Many of them once held dominion over huge parts of the world. Zamorak himself was a Mahjarrat, and he managed to achieve godhood!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: But now, they've all but vanished! Most will go through their whole lives without ever seeing one, and their most common appearance these days is in the spooky stories parents use to keep their children in line.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that's why you're so interested in Zemouregal?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Indeed. An actual Mahjarrat, and a very dangerous one at that. Ancient tomes can only teach us so much. If I want to learn everything there is to know about their race, it's going to take some more hands on work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about the danger?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Well that's just all the more reason for me to be here. If Zemouregal is planning a new attack on Varrock, an expert on his race might come in quite useful.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're an explorer, I'm guessing you travel a lot?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Indeed! From the frozen wastes of the north to the ancient tombs of the desert, I've seen it all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where do you call home?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I've lived in many places over the years, but I wouldn't really say I have a home. I guess here in Varrock would be the closest answer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like you have quite the history.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Oh yes! If nothing else, I've definitely lived a varied life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you weren't always an explorer?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Not at all. I've done all sorts of things in my time. In fact, at one point I was an adventurer like you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened? Some sort of life-changing injury?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Thankfully not. I just found that when it came to my work, it was the history I discovered that interested me more than the treasure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something else first.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Very well. I'll be here when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I think I'll pass.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Very well. If you change your mind, I'll be here.

sections.title: Talking to Elias White after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Ready to get back to it? My hope is we can pick up the trail left by those zombies right outside the inn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have that key we found?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I do indeed. Don't worry, I'll look after it until we need it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something else.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Very well. I'll meet you back at the Jolly Boar Inn.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I'll meet you back at the Jolly Boar Inn, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. So what was this tip-off you received?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: It's nothing much, but apparently one of the patrons in here was ranting and raving about zombies. Most just dismissed him as having had one too many, but I'm not so sure.

sections.title: Talking to Elias White while he is following the player

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: We need to try and pick up the trail left by those zombies.

sections.title: Talking to Elias White after discovering the footprints

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: We need to try and follow the trail left by those zombies.

sections.title: Inspecting the Plant north of the Jolly Boar Inn

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Look! Footprints. Let's see where they lead us.

sections.title: Inspecting the plant with the grubby key

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: A key? I wonder what it opens... Best we keep a hold of it and find out.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Why are you trying to destroy that? We need it. I can look after it for you if you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'll keep hold of it.

sections.title: Talking to Elias White while he is following the player, after finding the key

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: We need to try and follow the trail left by those zombies.

sections.title: Talking to Elias after opening the trapdoor

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Let's see what we can find down that trapdoor.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: A trapdoor! Now what do you reckon the chances are that we can unlock it with that key?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Only one way to find out...

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Right, shall we go in?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not ready yet.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Well let's not waste too much time. We need to see what's down there.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the trapdoor without Elias

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably fetch Elias first.

sections.title: Talking to Elias White in the Jolly Boar Inn after opening the trapdoor

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Ready to get back to it? We need to find out what's hiding down that trapdoor.

sections.title: Entering the open trapdoor while Elias is following

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Right, shall we go in?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Fascinating... What is this place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Then let's look around and find out!

sections.title: Talking to Elias after entering the dungeon

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Let's see if we can work out what this dungeon is for.

lines.speaker: Sharathteerk
lines.text: Zemouregal, this magical mist feels like it's corroding my insides. This seems like a bad idea...

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Nonsense! The mist has no side effects. Besides, it's essential for the new security system. Everyone needs to be imbued with it if they wish to pass through any doors or gates in the base.

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Now, what news do you bring from the north?

lines.speaker: Sharathteerk
lines.text: Your cousin grows unusually strong. We should consider allying ourselves to him, maybe even combine forces.

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Combine forces? I won't hear of it! Lucien would never enter into such an arrangement unless he were the one in control, and I have no desire to serve at his behest.

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Besides, Lucien isn't the only one with powerful resources...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that...?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Arrav. So the stories are true. The legendary hero of Avarrocka, now the slave of a disgusting necromancer.

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: After many long years, it is time for Arrav to take command of my zombie troops. And on that note, come, let us inspect the forces.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I see it's just as I suspected. Zemouregal is the one responsible for our zombie gatherings. We should inform the authorities in Varrock at once.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about the rest of this place? There could be more useful information to uncover here.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: True. Perhaps it would be wise for us to split up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a plan. You head to Varrock and let them know what we've found so far. I'll keep exploring.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Very well. I'll meet you in the palace once you're done. Be careful.

sections.title: Looking over the balcony after finishing the cutscene

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: You... You must leave this place. It's too... urgh...

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: ... too dangerous for the likes of you here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrav? But how are you... Are you fighting Zemouregal's control?

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: My body is... under his control. I can sometimes manage to speak when Zemouregal's concentration is elsewhere, but even then, it's... painful. My mind, however... gahh...

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: ... is still intact. He left it that way. He wanted me to feel the horror when I... urgh...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm so sorry, Arrav. There must be some way I can help you. What can I do?

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: My heart... He has it. That's how he... how he... arrgh...

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: I'm sorry, he calls to me. I must go. Please, leave... Leave before you...

lines.speaker: Sharathteerk
lines.text: I'm telling you, he may be close to finding it.

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Enough! I don't have time to worry about fairy tales.

lines.speaker: Sharathteerk
lines.text: But think of the power...

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Do you not think that I am all too aware of the power? You couldn't begin to imagine what I'd do to have it for myself! But I am also all too aware of what happens to those who try to claim it.

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: If that is the fate Lucien wishes for himself, then he is welcome to it. In the meantime, we will focus on more practical endeavours. This is our last chance before the time comes to head north.

lines.speaker: Sharathteerk
lines.text: Your forces are ready, Zemouregal.

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Then let us waste no more time. Begin the final preparations.

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Soon, the Mahjarrat will hold dominion over these lands once more. Varrock will be mine. Arrav cannot save his people this time. He can only watch as he himself seals their fate!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This isn't good. I'd better get to Varrock right away!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Ah, there you are. How did things go after I left you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not good. Zemouregal is nearly ready to begin another attack on Varrock.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Then it's just as I feared. I've already reported our discovery to Captain Rovin. Come, let's fill him in on everything else you've learnt.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: About time, adventurer. Your companion here tells me we have a zombie army on our doorstep!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: I'm afraid that's not all, Captain. [player name] has made some further discoveries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed. Zemouregal plans to have Arrav lead the zombies in an attack on Varrock, and he plans to do so very soon.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Zemouregal attacking Varrock again is our greatest fear and worst nightmare. And to think he now has Arrav under his control as well... What a horrifying fate for our greatest hero.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There may still be hope for Arrav. While spying on Zemouregal, I came face to face with him. He may serve Zemouregal, but not willingly. His mind is still his own.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: A zombie with his mind intact... Most zombies are mere husks, their souls having long departed their bodies. For one to still be sentient is highly unusual. Could it mean...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Oh, nothing to worry about now. Still, we may be able to work with this. What did Arrav have to say to you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He told me that Zemouregal has his heart.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: His heart? Interesting... Could that be how Zemouregal is controlling him?

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: What are you saying? Does this mean we might be able to save Arrav?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: In the long term, potentially. But this will require a fair amount of research. Something I fear we have little time for right now.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Well then what other options do we have?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Well thanks to our good friend here, we have the very shield that was used to stop Zemouregal the first time he attacked.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: The Shield of Arrav? But is it really any use? Despite our attempts, we've never been able to unlock its power. Could it be that the legends were exaggerated and that it's just an ordinary shield?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: To answer that, our best bet would likely be to seek out the creators of the shield: The Imcando Dwarves of Camdozaal.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Hmm... but are the Imcando not extinct now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not quite. There are still a few out there, including one in Camdozaal. Ramarno is his name.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Well then what are we waiting for? [player name], speak to this Ramarno and seek his advice on how to use the shield to defend our city. In the meantime, we will prepare our defences.

sections.title: Talking to Elias White or Captain Rovin after moving upstairs

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: You'd better head over to Camdozaal, [player name]. Time is not on our side.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Adventurer. Have you been to Camdozaal yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet. I'll be on my way shortly.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: About time, adventurer. Your companion here tells me we have a zombie army on our doorstep!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I access the Dream Theatre?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: You'll need to obtain some imbued barronite. Head to the mines and mine yourself a barronite deposit. Once you have that, imbue it using the core of a chaos golem from the golem workshop.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with a shield.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: A shield? What kind of shield?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Shield of Arrav. We've discovered that Zemouregal intends to attack the city of Varrock with a zombie army. It was the shield that stopped him last time.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, and you are hoping to use it to do so again. I wish I could help you, but I know little of the shield and its power. It was created far before my time.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Still, I may have a way for you to learn what you need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: The Sacred Forge of course!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how will a forge help me?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: The Sacred Forge is far more than a mere furnace! It is a powerful relic from a time when the gods still walked these lands.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Before Camdozaal's ruin, our sages would use the forge to guide our people. It is said the forge granted them access to the Dream Theatre, a place where they were blessed with visions of the past, present and future.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So how do I access this Dream Theatre?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: The Forge will need powering, but normal barronite won't work for this. You'll need to obtain some imbued barronite. With that, provided the forge deems you worthy, you will be able to enter the Dream Theatre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how do I get imbued barronite?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Head to the mines and mine yourself a barronite deposit. Once you have that, imbue it using the core of a chaos golem from the golem workshop.

sections.title: Talking to Ramarno after getting the imbued barronite

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I access the Dream Theatre?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I see you've got the imbued barronite. Just place it into the Sacred Forge. Provided the forge deems you worthy, you'll then be able to enter the Dream Theatre.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Arrav?

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: What is this? I feel... free. I can speak without pain. How is this possible?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We don't have much time. I'm talking to you from the Imcando Dream Theatre. I need to know how to use your shield.

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: The shield... of course. What do you need to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You used the shield to defeat Zemouregal's army before. How can we do it again?

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: It was not I who used the shield. When I faced Zemouregal, I cast the shield to the son of Avarrocka's founder. Only then did the zombies meet their doom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: It was his blood. The shield is tied to Avarrocka. Only one with the blood of the founder can use it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if I can find his descendant...

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: Then you can save my home.

lines.speaker: Arrav
lines.text: Wait... It's him! He knows you're here!

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: And what do we have here? Now, this is interesting... A snooping adventurer!

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: So you hope to stop me do you, you pathetic human wretch? Well my zombie army is at the walls of Varrock. You are already too late!

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Well? How did it go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know how to use the shield!

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Glad to hear it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Now I need to get back to Varrock as fast as possible. I just hope I'm not too late.

sections.title: Talking to Ramarno after the cutscene

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know how to use the shield!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh this is not good! I need to find Elias and Captain Rovin!

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Where did all these zombies come from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's an invasion by Zemouregal.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: The gods preserve us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm working on a plan to get rid of them.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: We're doomed!

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Whatever you want, adventurer, it will need to wait. In case you hadn't noticed, there are zombies all over the palace!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, Your Majesty. I'm trying to get rid of them.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Well get to it then, rather than standing there yapping at me!

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: What's going on? Where have all these zombies come from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're an army belonging to Zemouregal.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Zemouregal? Saradomin preserve us!

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: What are all these horrible, icky zombies doing in my nice, clean palace? You! You're an adventurer! Get out there and sort this out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on it.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: What's happening? Where did all these zombies come from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's an army of zombies belonging to Zemouregal.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Ah... that is bad news indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm working on a way to get rid of them, so fingers crossed.

lines.speaker: Ambassador Ferrnook
lines.text: Why are there zombies all over the place? Is this normal in Misthalin?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really. Normally they stick to caves and sewers.

lines.speaker: Ambassador Ferrnook
lines.text: Well I hope they get removed soon. This isn't very safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't you think you should try and get to somewhere safer?

lines.speaker: Ambassador Ferrnook
lines.text: Well, I'd prefer to wait it out, but if someone doesn't deal with these things soon, I may have to.

lines.speaker: Monk of Zamorak
lines.text: Ha! Mash those guards!

lines.speaker: Monk of Zamorak
lines.text: Hey, zombies! I'm on your side!

lines.speaker: Monk of Zamorak
lines.text: Let me out! I'll help with your invasion!

lines.speaker: Monk of Zamorak
lines.text: Let me out! I'm a bad guy too!

lines.speaker: Monk of Zamorak
lines.text: Oi, zombies! Care to let me out?

lines.speaker: Monk of Zamorak
lines.text: So, are any of you zombies gonna break me out?

sections.title: Talking to Elias or Rovin again

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: You're going to be needing this.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Have you managed to make any progress, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: You're back. Have you managed to make any progress?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, no.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Then you should get down to the library and talk to Reldo. If we don't get this situation under control, Varrock is doomed, and I'd very much prefer it if that could be avoided.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: About time you showed up! The zombies have breached the northern wall and are swarming the palace! We've managed to keep them from moving on the rest of Varrock for now, but we won't be able to do so forever!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: The situation isn't great, but all may not be lost. Did you manage to find out anything about how the shield works?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sort of. I discovered that only a descendant of Varrock's original founder can use the shield, but I'm not sure how we'd find someone of that lineage still alive today.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: The library would be your best bet. Get down there and see if Reldo can help you.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Oh, and you'd better take this.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Any luck with your search?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: That document on the desk in the corner should be of use. It listed the names of the elders who sent Arrav on his original quest for the shield.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: If you cross-reference the document with the latest census, you should hopefully be able to find their descendants.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Oh wonderful... he's gone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's gone?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Surok Magis. The teleport block should have stopped him escaping, but it seems he was able to use the zombies as a distraction and disable the spell.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: In hindsight, imprisoning a deadly mage in a library was maybe not a very well thought out plan, but it seems we have bigger issues to deal with for now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can say that again. Speaking of which, I need your help. I must find out if there are any living descendants of the founder of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: I'm normally the first person to chat about local history, but given the situation, is now really the best time for this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. This information may help us to defeat the zombies.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well in that case, I think I have what you need. There was a document on the desk in the corner listing the elders who sent Arrav on his original quest for the Shield of Arrav.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: One of those elders would have been the original founder of the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like a good start, but how would that help me track down their descendants?

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Notable families like that are unlikely to have changed their surnames over the years. If you consult the latest census, you'll hopefully find some matching names. You'll find it just next to the desk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's perfect! Thanks, Reldo.

sections.title: Talking to Elias or Rovin after talking to Reldo

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: You're going to be needing this.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Have you managed to make any progress, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: You're back. Have you managed to make any progress?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Reldo has given me a lead. Hopefully it will help us find someone who can use the shield.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Good. Follow this lead and see where it takes you, but be quick about it. If we don't get this situation under control, Varrock is doomed, and I'd very much prefer it if that could be avoided.

sections.title: Talking to Reldo after finding the list

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Any luck with your search for those descendants?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so. I have some potential contenders at least.

lines.speaker: Reldo
lines.text: Well let's hope this works then. Things are starting to look a little bit problematic.

sections.title: Talking to Elias or Rovin after finding the list

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: You're going to be needing this.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Have you managed to make any progress, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: You're back. Have you managed to make any progress?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some contenders for the descendant of Varrock's original founder. I now just need to work out who the right one is.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Try giving the shield to them. Whoever the right person is, the shield should respond to them in some way.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: And be quick about it. If we don't get this situation under control, Varrock is doomed, and I'd very much prefer it if that could be avoided.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Where did all these zombies come from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's an invasion by Zemouregal.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: The gods preserve us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'm working on a plan to get rid of them. You might be able to help by using the Shield of Arrav.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: The Shield of Arrav?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. A descendant of an original elder of Avarrocka can apparently use it to stop the zombies. You're one of those descendants, although I'm not sure if you're the right one.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Well, I suppose anything is worth a try.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Nothing seems to be happening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It must be someone else who's the right descendant then.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Oh no... What if this doesn't work? We're doomed! We're doomed!

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Nothing seems to be happening. What made you think I was descended from an original elder of Avarrocka?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well your surname is Prysin. It matches the name of an elder called Khom Prysin.

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: But I'm not actually a blood descendant of the Prysins. My great-grandfather, Wally, was adopted into the family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... So, are there any genuine Prysins around?

lines.speaker: Sir Prysin
lines.text: Well, none with the name Prysin. The last descendant of the Prysin line was a lady named Rhee Prysin. She married into the Fitzharmon family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As in Dimintheis Fitzharmon? Sounds like I'd better pay him a visit.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: What's going on? Where have all these zombies come from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're an army belonging to Zemouregal.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Zemouregal? Saradomin preserve us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: All might not be lost. You might be able to help us by using the Shield of Arrav.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: But I don't know how to use the shield... No one does.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a reason to believe that you are descended from an original elder of Avarrocka, which might mean that you have the ability to use it. Here, hold it and see if anything happens.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Nothing special seems to be happening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then it must be someone else who's descended from the correct elder.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Well then you need to find them before these zombies kill us all!

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Nothing special seems to be happening. Why do you think I'm descended from an original elder of Avarrocka, anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well your surname is Raispher. It matches the name of an elder called Dathero Raispher.

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Smart thinking, but also flawed. I'm not actually a blood descendant of the Raisphers. My great-grandfather was adopted into the family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... So, are there any genuine Raisphers around?

lines.speaker: Aeonisig Raispher
lines.text: Well, none with the name Raispher. The last descendant of the Raispher line was a lady named Lucinda Raispher. She married into the Fitzharmon family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As in Dimintheis Fitzharmon? Sounds like I'd better pay him a visit.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Whatever you want, adventurer, it will need to wait. In case you hadn't noticed, there are zombies all over the palace!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I might have a solution to that. We have the Shield of Arrav.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: No one has figured out how to unlock the powers of the shield in hundreds of years! Are we so desperate that we are relying on legends?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It might be that you yourself can use it. I have a reason to believe that you are descended from an original elder of Avarrocka.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Well of course! Would you expect anything less of the man who is king?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here, hold the shield and see if anything happens.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: It doesn't seem to be doing anything special.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It must be someone else who is descended from the correct elder then.

lines.speaker: King Roald
lines.text: Well then you'd better get out there and find them! These zombies need to be stopped!

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Hello, do you need any help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with a shield.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: A shield? Sounds like my area. What do you need?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not your skills as a Blacksmith I need. It's to do with the zombie attack on the palace!

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Oh, I see. What do you need me to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have reason to believe that you are descended from an original elder of Avarrocka, which might mean you have the ability to use the Shield of Arrav to defeat the zombies.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: The Shield of Arrav? Sounds a bit unlikely to me, but I'll give it a go.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: It doesn't seem to be doing anything exciting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It must be a different descendant that I'm after then.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Well good luck finding them. Last thing we need is those zombies moving on the rest of the city.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: It doesn't seem to be doing anything exciting. Why do you think I was descended from an original elder of Avarrocka, anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well your surname is Ravitz. It matches the name of an elder called Ramone Ravitz.

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: Ah... I'm not actually a blood descendant of the Ravitzes. My great-grandfather was adopted into the family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... So, are there any genuine Ravitzes around?

lines.speaker: Horvik
lines.text: I'm the only one I know of with the Ravitz surname. The last descendant of the Ravitz line was a lady named Shauna Ravitz. She married into the Fitzharmon family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As in Dimintheis Fitzharmon? Sounds like I'd better pay him a visit.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Welcome to the museum of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with the zombie invasion.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I'm flattered, but I'm sure I don't have the combat prowess for those kinds of shenanigans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe not normally, but the Shield of Arrav might change that.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: I'm not sure the shield, nice as it is, will make that much difference.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I have reason to believe that you are descended from an original elder of Avarrocka, which might mean you have the ability to use the shield. Here, hold it and see if anything happens.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Well, I've held it a few times in the course of my work, but I'll give it a go.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Nothing special seems to be happening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It must be someone else who is descended from the correct elder then.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Well I wish you luck in finding them.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Nothing special seems to be happening. Why do you think I'm descended from an original elder of Varrock, anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, your surname is Halen. It matches the name of an elder called Marv Halen.

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: Now that is smart thinking, but I'm not actually a blood descendant of the original Halens. My great-grandfather was adopted into the family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... So, are there any genuine Halens around?

lines.speaker: Curator Haig Halen
lines.text: The last descendant of the Halen line that I know of was a lady named Edwina. She married into the Fitzharmon family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As in Dimintheis Fitzharmon? Sounds like I'd better pay him a visit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Romeo! I need to speak to you. A zombie army has invaded the palace and you might be the only one who's able to stop them.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ooh, do you really think I could? I've always wanted to be a hero!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm not sure. We might be able to find out with this shield though.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Shield? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have reason to believe that you are descended from an original elder of Avarrocka, which might mean that you can use the Shield of Arrav to defeat the zombies. Here, hold it and see if anything happens.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Ooh, okay, that sounds fun!

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh... Nothing's happening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess it must be a different descendant that I'm after.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: That's a shame. I would have quite liked to have been the one to use it.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh... Nothing's happening. Are you sure it was me who was meant to be using it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it wasn't a guarantee, but your surname matches that of an elder called Ramone Gontamue.

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Oh, well I'm not actually a real descendant of the Gontamues. My great-grandfather was adopted into the family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... So, are there any genuine Gontamues around?

lines.speaker: Romeo
lines.text: Not exactly. The last descendant of the Gontamue line was Lilith Gontamue. She married into the Fitzharmon family.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As in Dimintheis Fitzharmon? Sounds like I'd better pay him a visit.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: You have my eternal gratitude for all you have done in saving my family's honour, adventurer. Perhaps some day my sons will overcome their differences.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with the zombie invasion.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Well, I suppose I could help, but I am getting on a bit now. I'm not quite the warrior I used to be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm hoping the Shield of Arrav may help a little.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Ah, yes, I know of the shield's legend. I don't understand what that has to do with me though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have reason to believe that you're descended from one of the original elders of Avarrocka, which might mean you have the ability to use the shield.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: That doesn't sound entirely convincing, but I'll give it a go.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Well, that is impressive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you might be the one! Quick, let's get to the palace!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Dimintheis.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: I will not forget what you did for my family, [player name]. Those 500,000 coins have allowed us to put food on the table.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's no problem.

sections.title: Talking to Dimintheis before completing the cutscene

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: You have my eternal gratitude for all you have done in saving my family's honour, adventurer. Perhaps some day my sons will overcome their differences.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with the zombie invasion.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: You want me to use the shield? It seems to be responding to me in some way! Is this what you were expecting?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so! Quick, let's get to the palace!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Dimintheis.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: I will not forget what you did for my family, [player name]. Those 500,000 coins have allowed us to put food on the table.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's no problem.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Die, zombie scum!

lines.speaker: Monk of Zamorak
lines.text: Hurrah! Free at last.

lines.speaker: Monk of Zamorak
lines.text: Erm.

lines.speaker: Queen Ellamaria
lines.text: Stay back! I won't let you eat me!

lines.speaker: Sharathteerk
lines.text: Zemouregal... The forces... What do we do?

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: What do we do? We flee, you fool!

lines.speaker: Sharathteerk
lines.text: But all the work...

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Do you have a better suggestion? Are you hiding some secret to overcoming such an astounding disaster?

lines.speaker: Sharathteerk
lines.text: No...

lines.speaker: Zemouregal
lines.text: Then recall Arrav at once! There is nothing more to be gained here. It is time for us to head north.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: It seems the shield worked better than anyone could have hoped for. From what we can tell, not a single zombie remains! It was a close one, but thanks to you, Varrock is saved!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wouldn't have been possible without Dimintheis.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Indeed. I will ensure the King is made aware of his contribution.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any word on what happened to Zemouregal?

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: There's been no sign of him so far. We'll keep an eye out, but I suspect he'll have gone to ground.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And he'll have Arrav with him.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: We may not have been able to save Arrav today, but thanks to your discoveries, we now have a lead to follow. It will take me some time, but once I have something on this heart business, I'll be in touch.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: And on that note, I intend to get started right away. Until we meet again.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Well once again, adventurer, on behalf of Varrock, you have my thanks. Today's victory will go down in history.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Now, you'd best be on your way. I have lots of work to do, and I'm sure you have better places to be than here.

sections.title: Talking to Captain Rovin after Elias departs

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Well once again, adventurer, on behalf of Varrock, you have my thanks. Today's victory will go down in history.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Oh, it's you. Thank you again for your help in defeating the zombie army.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're welcome.

lines.speaker: Captain Rovin
lines.text: Now, you must excuse me. I have lots to do.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Hello again, adventurer. I hope that Shield of Arrav business marks the start of something big for my family. Maybe it will help me finally get out of this hole!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Dimintheis.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: I will not forget what you did for my family, [player name]. Those 500,000 coins have allowed us to put food on the table.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's no problem.

lines.speaker: Dimintheis
lines.text: Combine that with the whole Shield of Arrav business and things are really starting to look up for us!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Ah, hello there, [player name]. Good to see you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you, Elias. So what brings you all the way out here?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Well it turns out the desert was the best place to begin my research into Arrav and his heart. Not too surprising of course, given the burial practices here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how's that going so far?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Pretty well. In fact, I think I'm getting close to something. It's related to that old mastaba up on the plateau. I reckon it might contain the knowledge we need to help old Arrav out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you, Elias. So you're back out here again?

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Indeed. Turns out the desert was the best place to begin my research into Arrav and his heart. Not too surprising of course, given the burial practices here.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: In fact, as you might recall, I was here before to look into that old mastaba up on the plateau.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I remember. You said it might contain important information on the Mahjarrat.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Indeed I did. And I reckon it might also contain the knowledge we need to help old Arrav out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excellent!

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Now, I still have quite a bit of preparatory work to do before we consider heading inside, but once that's done, I'll be in touch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good. I'd best leave you to your work.

lines.speaker: Elias White
lines.text: Until next time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Hello there, welcome to Varrock Museum! Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some information that might be of use in your displays.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: That's grand! We're always glad to have more facts. Now, tell me what you know...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, we got rid of the marauding zombies.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: That's handy. Zombies do tend to leave horrible muddy footprints around the place. So what actually happened with them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, Zemouregal the Mahjarrat sent his zombie army against Varrock. However, we figured out how to stop him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found out that the descendant of the founder of Varrock could use the shield to stop the zombie army. It turns out that Dimintheis was that descendant, so he was able to defeat the zombies.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: My, my, that is all very interesting. I shall make sure the display is updated at once! Display number 24, 4th Age, year 700-800.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: Oh, and before I forget, we found this old lamp lying around. Since you've done such a sterling job, we'd like you to have it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, random bits of tat, thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Historian Minas
lines.text: You're welcome!
